attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who commits early to long-range moves, prioritizing positioning for secondary objectives (jerseys, team goals) over outright stage wins.
team_tactics: EF Education deploys him in breakaways with strategic objectives beyond stage victory; team willing to sacrifice win opportunities to secure classification jerseys through his positioning.
terrain_preference: Versatile across terrain types — capable in both hilly classics (Bretagne) and Grand Tour breakaway stages. Functions as climbing domestique with unexpected finishing speed in one-day races.
vulnerability: Lacks outright winning speed against pure finishers; relegated to support roles suggest limitations in sustained high-end power for stage victories from breakaways.
key_patterns: Commits to early breakaways with tactical objectives beyond stage wins (2 instances)
Shows resilience and determination: fought back after crash to rejoin breakaway group
Delivers strong finishing speed for a climbing domestique role in classics racing
Team prioritizes his jersey/classification positioning over individual stage victory opportunities
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta performance showed tactical maturity and resilience (crash recovery), building on 2022 classics success
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 2
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 2
